subject: Computer Networking
title: CDNs: Content Closer to You
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: CDNs: Content Closer to You.

hook.text: A user in Tokyo loads a site hosted in New York. The page is fast. How? The content was already in Tokyo.

buildup.text: A CDN copies popular files — images, scripts, videos — to servers all around the world.

discovery.text: When you request a file, the CDN serves it from the nearest location. Less distance means less latency.

twist.text: CDNs also absorb traffic spikes. If a site goes viral, the CDN handles the load instead of one server.

climax.text: Netflix, YouTube, and major news sites all depend on CDNs. Without them, the internet would feel glacial.

punchline.text: CDNs bring content to you instead of you to content.

quiz.question: What is the main benefit of a CDN?
options: Serving content from a server geographically closer to the user
